channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: 🔴 Syria: Explosions heard in Damascus during French president Macron's visit
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Explosions heard in Damascus during French president Macron's visit
item.description: Explosions ⁠were heard ​in ​Damascus on ​Tuesday where French President Emmanuel Macron is currently visiting.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump casts Democrats as red 'menace' with McCarthyist rhetoric ahead of US midterms
item.description: The US midterm election campaign is gaining momentum, and US President Donald Trump is reviving Cold War era rhetoric to peg the Democratic Party as extremists and a red "menace".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Live: Marine Le Pen’s presidential hopes at stake with key French court ruling
item.description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen faces a key ruling Tuesday with a Paris appeals court ruling on an EU parliament funds misuse case, which will determine if she can run in the 2027 presidential elections. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Oil tanker hit by 'unknown projectile' off Omani coast near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A tanker was struck Monday by an "unknown projectile" off the coast of Oman, in the Strait of Hormuz region, according to the British maritime agency UKMTO.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup: Spain knock out Portugal and Cristiano Ronaldo
item.description: Spain have qualified for the World Cup quarter-finals after beating Portugal (1-0). Cristiano Ronaldo has played his final World Cup match.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: Live: Ukraine launches over 400 drones towards Moscow ahead of NATO summit
item.description: Ukraine launched more than 430 drones towards Moscow overnight, the capital’s mayor said Tuesday. One person was killed in a Ukrainian strike in Russia’s Belgorod region after Russian attacks killed dozens in Ukraine. NATO leaders are set to announce tens of billions of dollars in defence deals in Ankara ahead of the summit, as European allies seek to boost military spending. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Belgium thump USA to reach quarter-finals and knock co-hosts out of tournament
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Belgium thump ragged USA to end co-hosts' World Cup dream and set up Spain clash
item.description: Charles De Ketalaere scored twice as Belgium brought the United States' World Cup party to a shattering halt on Monday, thrashing the tournament co-hosts 4-1 to set up a quarter-final showdown with Spain. The buildup to the match was overshadowed by a row over US President Donald Trump's extraordinary intervention to earn a red-card reprieve for striker Folarin Balogun.
item.source: © Alex Grimm, Getty Images via AFP
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Kylian Mbappé condemns Paraguayan senator over 'despicable' racist abuse
item.description: Kylian Mbappé condemned a Paraguayan senator on Monday for making racist comments about him after France's World Cup last-16 victory over Paraguay, calling her "despicable" and "not worthy" of her position. The remarks sparked widespread condemnation from French officials, including President Emmanuel Macron.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Israeli parliament backs first step towards October 7 inquiry
item.description: Israel's parliament approved the first reading of a bill on Monday to establish a commission of inquiry into the security failings surrounding Hamas's October 7, 2023 attack, though opposition lawmakers boycotted the vote, arguing the proposed body would lack independence.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Belgium thrash USA to reach quarter-finals and knock out co-costs
item.description: Charles De Ketalaere scored twice as Belgium thumped the US 4-1 in their World Cup last-16 clash to end the co-hosts' World Cup dream. The buildup to the match was overshadowed by a row over US President Donald Trump's extraordinary intervention to earn a red-card reprieve for striker Folarin Balogun. Read our blog to see how the match unfolded.
item.source: © Alex Grimm, Getty Images via AFP
item.dc:creator: Benjamin DODMAN

item.title: Russian missile and drone strikes kill 24 in Kyiv ahead of NATO summit
item.description: Russia launched a deadly barrage of missiles and drones on Kyiv on Monday, killing at least 24 people and wounding more than 100 in its second major strike on the capital in a week. The attack came on the eve of a NATO summit, where President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to press allies for stronger air defence support.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Spain edge Portugal with late Merino goal to reach quarter-finals
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Merino strikes late as Spain edge Portugal, drawing curtain on Ronaldo's World Cup career
item.description: Substitute Mikel Merino scored an injury-time winner as European champions Spain beat Iberian rivals Portugal in a tense last-16 encounter to book a place in the World Cup quarter-finals and put an end to Cristiano Ronaldo's career on football's biggest stage.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: ‘Restraining order needed’: Trump shares doctored Meloni meme to reignite feud
item.description: On the eve of a key NATO summit, Donald Trump reignited his feud with Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni by sharing a doctored image on Truth Social, captioned “restraining order needed.” The altered image is based on genuine G7 footage but changes Meloni's facial expression to make her appear enamoured with Trump. This new jab follows weeks of bitter public clashes between the two leaders, following Trump’s claims she "begged" him for a photo.
item.dc:creator: Vedika BAHL

item.title: Love, deception and the real lives of Nigeria's romance fraudsters
item.description: In tonight's edition, France pulls all of its diplomats out of Burkina Faso in a tit-for-tat move after Ougadougou cut ties with Paris last month. Also, in Tanzania, young people are planning on taking to the streets for an anti-government protest on Tuesday. And Lagos's Yahoo Boys are behind a lucrative but heartbreaking industry of online love scams: after his mother was sucked in, journalist Carlos Barragan uncovered a complex and uncomfortable web of loneliness, deceit, and betrayal.
item.dc:creator: Georja CALVIN-SMITH

item.title: Death toll from Venezuela quakes rises to 3,535 as more than 17,000 left homeless
item.description: The death toll from the back-to-back earthquakes that hit Venezuela on June 24 has risen to 3,535, official figures released on Monday showed. More than 17,000 people have been left homeless by the twin disasters.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: OPEC+ raises output but cartel's future hangs in balance
item.description: A handful of OPEC+ countries have agreed to increase their output targets from August but the cartel is fighting for its survival after the UAE's departure. A French company is using the Seine to cool down Paris' buildings. And, Sky says it's buying ITV's broadcast channels and streaming service for £1.6bn.
item.dc:creator: Camille KNIGHT

item.title: World Cup 2026 : Watching Mexico-England in one Boston's Hispanic outskirts
item.description: Mexico-England ended in a 3-2 victory for the Three Lions at the Azteca Stadium in Mexico City. In Chelsea, on the outskirts of Boston, the Mexican community turned out in force to watch the game.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France's Macron in first post-Assad visit to Damascus : why the Syria stopover ?
item.description: As Iran buries its Supreme Guide who was killed last February, and Hamas started to disarm in Gaza, Emmanuel Macron is going to Damascus, for the first visit of a French President since Nicolas Sarkozy met the deposed President Bachar Al-Assad in 2009. The timing of this visit raises questions, as well as the objectives of the French President when meeting his Syrian counterpart Ahmed Al-Shareh.
item.dc:creator: Guillaume MERCIER

item.title: Portugal vs Spain: Merino scores in injury time to send La Roja to quarter-finals
item.description: Substitute ​Mikel Merino scored in ​added time as Spain edged Portugal 1-0 to reach the World Cup quarter-finals on Monday, extending La Roja's run without conceding ​a goal and putting an end to Cristiano Ronaldo's career on football's biggest stage. Read our blog to see how the match unfolded.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin DODMAN

item.title: Frenchman convicted in missing body case confesses to killing his wife, lawyer says
item.description: Cédric Jubillar, a painter and plasterer who was sentenced last October for killing his wife Delphine, has finally confessed to the murder from his prison cell, his lawyer said Monday. The 38-year-old said he was ready to tell investigators the location of his wife's body, which was never found.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Death toll from DR Congo Ebola outbreak passes 500 as healthcare workers threaten strike
item.description: The deadly Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has killed more than 500 people, the UN health agency's figures showed Monday. Healthcare workers in Ituri province, the heart of the outbreak, on Sunday issued a 24-hour notice threatening to strike if the government did not improve their working conditions and pay them promised benefits.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
